name: Lumentum Holdings
window: Q3 FY26 · May 06 → Jul 16, 2026 (Jun-Q prints 11 Aug)

events.text: Record Q3 FY26 print: revenue US$808.4M (+90% YoY, above the guide midpoint); non-GAAP GM 47.9% (+540bps QoQ, +1,270bps YoY); op margin 32.2%; non-GAAP EPS US$2.37 (GAAP US$1.50). Cash US$3.17B post-NVIDIA.

EML supply-demand imbalance ">30%" (undershipping ~30% of customer demand and widening even as capacity is added); targeting >50% EML unit growth by Dec-Q 2026; record EML shipments +100% YoY. Pump-laser constraint cited as the "biggest… somewhat unanticipated" bottleneck.

Q4 FY26 guide: revenue US$960M–1.01B, non-GAAP op margin 35–36%, non-GAAP EPS US$2.85–3.05 (~102M shares). OCS: multi-year, multibillion-dollar purchase agreement; backlog well past US$400M, the majority shipping in 2027, three customers.
BofA (Vivek Arya) Neutral, PT US$1,100 — best growth in the sector but "upside well priced in" (CY26/27E +18%/+9%).

"Scale-across" pump-laser components today at mid-US$10s (~US$40–60M/qtr), guided to 3–4x output/revenue over the next 4–6 quarters; LITE holds 70–80% of the pump-laser market and similar in ITLAs, "essentially the kingmaker in terms of allocation" — pump supply behind demand by ~50–60%, more than EMLs, with mid-double-digit % price increases.

LITE reached the "front of the line" at the primary transceiver customer (Google), with 100% share on some key 1.6T SKUs near-term (more temporal than the OCS moat — Innolight/Coherent catch up in 1–2 quarters). Google's new Virgo back-end network raises optics attach per TPU.

LITE feeding ~20% of the transceiver mix with its own CW lasers (in response to a supplier's CW-laser decommit), borrowing from InP capacity and modestly moderating EML revenue growth; transceiver manufacturing/margin is still the weak spot.

OCS inflection in the Sep-Q quantified: OCS ~US$70M in the June quarter steps up to ~US$170M/qtr in 1H-FY27 (≈US$100M of sequential growth from OCS alone), making September the primary revenue driver; Dec-Q OCS ≥US$200M, ideally ~US$250M.
JPM (Meyers): "Lots of irons in the fire; execution the concern" — execution is the central worry.

Scale-across demand is not just Ciena/Meta — Nokia, Cisco, Molex and Ciena are all placing orders, splitting the network back-end across a DCI framework. The charge-pump line (~US$30M/qtr run-rate) doubles next quarter, at margins better than even EMLs. First scale-up CPO demand (NVL576/Feynman) starts around early cal-28.
@ResearchQf: stock "most extended vs 4yr avg", 20–40% correction plausible.
MS: "Sold out through 2028" possible; LITE expects to fill its entire order book through 2028 within two quarters.

Hurlston: "there's no demand problem, which is an enviable situation… our issue is execution". At ~US$805M in Q3 they are ~halfway to the OFC target of US$2B/qtr revenue / 40% OM in 18–24 months. Substrate "mostly under control" via Sumitomo but "tighter than I'd like"; equipment (reactors, etch) now the inhibitor.
Barclays (Rand): sentiment "a lot more mixed than a few months ago… pushback on huge EPS numbers".

NVDA scale-out CPO adoption now tracking >50% vs ~40% a few quarters ago. Non-NVIDIA customers seeking to lock up the remaining Greensboro fab capacity, pressuring NVDA to make further fab investment for visibility.

JEF (Beavington) flagged a CPO OIO pushout to 2030-31 on interposer overheating; MS (Marshall) and JPM (Chatterjee) call CPO concerns "overblown" and a buy-the-dip. FundaAI framed Hurlston's comments at Mizuho as "a supply problem, not demand".

Expert (Bob): the CPO bottleneck is the ultra-high-power laser — only Lumentum and Coherent are a reliable source (AVGO doesn't show up in near-term designs). One external laser feeds multiple ports → higher ASP, suppliers 'essentially two, possibly three'. Most early CPO designs use external laser sources — reinforces the LITE/COHR moat on the UHP light source.

'DC Capex Ramping to >$1.4T in 2028E' — reiterate Outperform, PT $1,100 (~33x F28E P/E; price $802.01). LITE a 'critical supplier' (with CRDO) to the AI DC-capex ramp (cons $1.2T 2027E / >$1.4T 2028E; ~$50B/GW; 106GW 2026-30E ≈ $5.3T). Sole-source NVDA CPO UHP-laser supplier + OCS ramping at 3 hyperscalers; >50% AI EML unit share at 100G, holding ~50% at 200G/lane.

'Bringing More Light to Scale-Up Networks' — scale-up market ~$70bn by 2030 (>4x prior est); copper stays longer, CPO eventual (meaningful at NVDA Feynman — small 2028, stronger 2029+), optical sell-offs 'overdone'. LITE a 'clearest beneficiary' of optical into scale-up (with COHR/GLW); model rebuilt for CPO %, 2027E rev $5,374M→$5,668M, adj EPS $17.31→$18.37; FY28 adj-EPS sensitivity ~$22.6-$31.0. Scale-up optical ships late 2027, in-market early 2028.

'InP Supply Constraint Will Likely Persist' — Buy/PT $1,100 nc, opens an upside Catalyst Watch into the Jun-Q print (event 11 Aug). Debuts an InP (EML+CW) supply model: supply below demand through 2028 (EML gap 33%/20%/11% 2026-28; CW widening to 19%/55% 2027/28; chips ~280M→~1.1B 2025→28E; 6-inch the lever at 4.36x/wafer). LITE = leader in 100G/200G EML + UHP lasers → pricing power; models a further +15% FY27E price increase → FY27E/28E EPS +6%/+5% (next-FY EPS $20.03→$21.18). NVDA still a low-single-digit-% customer.

cells: Record EML; demand > supply into CY26
New customers ramping; volume fiscal Q4
